summary: codebuff-cli impersonates CodebuffAI and drops an opaque binary from an unrelated GitHub account with TLS verification disabled
details: Package name and README impersonate the legitimate `codebuff` CLI by CodebuffAI (README instructs `npm install -g codebuff`). The bin script `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` fetches an executable from `https://api.github.com/repos/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/latest` — a personal GitHub account unrelated to the CodebuffAI publisher — using the mutable `releases/latest` tag with no hash or signature verification, writes it under `~/.codebuff/bin`, sets mode 0755, and executes it on every CLI invocation. The wrapper additionally launches the dropped binary with `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0` in its environment, disabling TLS certificate validation for any HTTPS traffic the child performs and enabling MITM of further code or data the binary fetches. The README also documents the backend as a randomly-slugged Vercel preview URL (`fireworks-api-backend-clviguvqt-sjdjdiejdrirhdkjejs-projects.vercel.app`) rather than codebuff.com, consistent with attacker-controlled relay infrastructure for an AI coding agent that processes user source code. Three independent block signals stack: typosquat impersonation of a known publisher, mutable-ref dropper from a non-publisher personal GitHub account with no integrity check, and deliberate TLS-verification disablement in the spawned child.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
